text: This is a case of laparoscopic management of the dermoid cyst.
The video shows a laparoscopic view of the left ovary in a young girl, revealing a dermoid cyst approximately four centimeters in size.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to carefully open the outer cortex of the ovary, pulling the instrument anteriorly to avoid puncturing the dermoid cyst.
The surgeon makes a long incision over the anti-meso-ovarian border of the ovary, preparing to access the dermoid cyst within the ovarian tissue.
The surgeon uses a harmonic device to separate the dermoid cyst from the surrounding ovarian tissue without causing bleeding, ensuring the cyst remains intact during the laparoscopic procedure.
The surgeon begins the cystectomy using a Maryland dissector and a grasper, carefully manipulating the instruments to separate the dermoid cyst from the surrounding ovarian tissue.
The surgeon uses the Maryland forceps to open its jaws, creating a proper dissection plane around the dermoid cyst in the left ovary, ensuring the ovary does not rupture during enucleation.
During the laparoscopic ovarian cystectomy, especially for dermoid cyst, we have to take utmost care that it should not rupture. But if it is ruptured also, it is not a big concern.
The surgeon performs thorough copious lavage to clean the entire abdominal cavity, ensuring no remnants of dermoid cyst content are left behind.
The surgeon carefully extracts a small ovarian cyst using laparoscopic instruments, ensuring it is removed intact without rupture.

The surgeon has identified the dissection plane and is carefully separating the ovarian cyst from the surrounding tissue using a Maryland dissector and grasper, ensuring the cyst remains intact during the enucleation process.
Embedrexity is required in this case.
The surgeon carefully holds the cyst with one hand while stripping the ovary with the other, ensuring the cyst does not slip and avoiding the use of a traumatic grasper.
The surgeon uses atraumatic graspers to carefully manipulate the cyst, minimizing the risk of rupture during extraction.

The surgeon completes the enucleation of the cyst, carefully separating the remaining tags of ovarian tissue from the cyst. The ovary is then examined for any bleeding or residual dermoid tissue, with no abnormalities observed.

The surgeon uses a 10mm cannula to introduce an endo bag, placing the intact dermoid cyst inside. The cyst is then slowly pulled into the cannula, and both the cannula and endo bag are removed from the surgical site.
An assistant holds the endobag containing the cyst while the surgeon uses ovum forceps to rupture the cyst inside the bag, ensuring the endobag remains intact during the process.
Thank you very much for watching this video of the laparoscopic management of dermarcyst. Have a nice day. Thank you very much.
